title: The Third Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: June 26, 2022

## LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Third Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time, falling within the long green season of the church calendar. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would have been 2 Kings 2:1-2, 6-14 (Elijah's ascension); Psalm 77:1-2, 11-20; Galatians 5:1, 13-25 (Christian freedom); and Luke 9:51-62 (Jesus setting his face toward Jerusalem). This represents the beginning of Luke's "Travel Narrative," a theologically significant passage about discipleship and commitment.

## EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the interim period between its 2021 General Convention (held in Baltimore) and the upcoming 79th General Convention scheduled for July 2022 in Austin, Texas—just one week after this sermon. The Denver Diocese was operating under Bishop Konde's leadership (Robert Imhoff was the Bishop of Colorado). National Episcopal attention was focused on pre-Convention discussions regarding LGBTQ+ inclusion and various theological resolutions being debated.

## DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill area where St. John's Cathedral is located were preparing for summer events and Colorado's mid-year political cycle. Colorado had recently been in the news regarding its abortion landscape, with the state being a refuge destination following the Dobbs decision overturning Roe v. Wade (decided June 24, 2022—just two days before this sermon). Local news cycles were heavily focused on this Supreme Court ruling's implications for Colorado.

## NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The dominant news story was the Supreme Court's Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization decision on June 24, 2022, overturning Roe v. Wade and returning abortion regulation to individual states—this would have been the primary conversation in Denver churches that weekend. The January 6 House Select Committee was holding prime-time televised hearings presenting evidence about the Capitol riot, with a major hearing scheduled for June 28, 2022. Gas prices remained elevated, with national average hovering around $5 per gallon, and inflation was a persistent economic concern under the Biden administration.

## INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine was entering its fifth month with no resolution in sight; Kyiv had recently come under renewed Russian artillery fire and drone attacks. The World Health Organization was tracking expanding monkeypox cases across multiple continents, which was beginning to receive significant media attention. The UK was experiencing a political crisis with Boris Johnson facing multiple resignations from his cabinet over various scandals.

## CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a state of acute political and moral polarization, with the Dobbs decision crystallizing deep divisions over reproductive rights, federalism, and religious values. The mood was one of uncertainty and heightened stakes—the congregation at St. John's would have included both those grieving the abortion decision and those celebrating it. The summer of 2022 carried anxiety about economic stability, ongoing pandemic aftereffects (though COVID was receding from daily consciousness), and intense partisan divisions that made consensus difficult even within faith communities.
